text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Yaeno Muteki is struggling to quell the fiery aggression in her. That's
our first hurdle, so I've been brainstorming solutions.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: And now, I think I've got a lead.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text: You want to learn more about Kongo Yaegaki martial arts?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The day I first met her, Yaeno Muteki was repeating forms to calm her
mind before the tryouts—just like she said she did as a child.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I have no doubt that the source of her usual composure lies in Kongo
Yaegaki teachings, so I'm sure it'll help find a way to control her
blaze.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
Well, I gather this interest in it has something to do with suppressing
my blaze.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text: ...I see your train of thought. Follow me.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
Master. Adept. We are ready for the lecture. We humbly ask your
guidance.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandmother
text_block_list.text:
It's a pleasure to have you, Trainer. We're flattered to hear that
you've taken an interest in the Kongo Yaegaki school.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandmother
text_block_list.text:
I believe seeing it in action will explain more than words. I hope you'll
enjoy today's lesson.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: Soon after...

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text: Sei! HAH!

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandmother
text_block_list.text:
Good, that's the idea. Next, try to punch without tipping your whole
body's balance forward. Instead of raising your head up, let your
body relax and dangle, like a puppet on a string.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text: Yaeno, let your waist sit lower! The motion should be sharp. Again!

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text: Understood! Thank you, Master!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
While the forms I'm practicing are admittedly much simpler than
Yaeno Muteki's, I complete a basic training session, getting an
overview of the school's style.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
...That said, trying all those new moves has me exhausted. I rest for a
bit, watching Yaeno Muteki practice.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text: Trainer, may I have a word?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Yaeno Muteki continues the lesson with her grandmother, while her
grandfather—master of the Kongo Yaegaki school—softly approaches
me.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text: Allow me to thank you for looking after my granddaughter.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text:
I can guess what brought you here. The aggressive blaze in her is
affecting her racing, and she seeks to extinguish it with the tranquility
of water. Am I correct?

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text:
I thought as much. Her posture is stiff—her motions, shallow. I sense
fear in her.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text:
She's been scared of the blaze for so long, she has a tendency to
overcompensate, trying too hard to be still as water. However,
Trainer, it's not so simple...

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text: Master both blaze fierce and water still—a guardian you will be.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text:
Kongo Yaegaki is a school that emphasizes defense, you see. The
saying about fire and water speaks to the importance of balancing
aggressive and passive stances.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text:
Hot-headed fighting spirit alone leads to hasty, sloppy actions,
burning energy that could have been used to protect.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text:
Likewise, having only the composure of tranquil water is no better, for
overthinking causes hesitation. By the time one moves, it may be too
late.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text:
That is why the concepts of "blaze fierce" and "water still" are taught
in tandem. A guardian must act immediately, but intelligently.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text:
In Kongo Yaegaki, we use set forms, called katas, in order to channel
wild, fire-like power into calculated, fluid motions.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text:
I hope that makes sense. Extinguishing Yaeno's fire with tranquility is
not the answer.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno's Grandfather
text_block_list.text: I believe that is true for both martial arts and racing. What she lacks is balance. That is what she must endeavor to master.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
So you'd like to brainstorm ways to balance blaze fierce and water
still in racing? Hmm...

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
Well, as you've already heard from my grandfather, Kongo Yaegaki
uses katas to channel wild power.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text: That begs the question—what is the racing equivalent of a kata?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
In martial arts, katas are motions designed to combat specific
situations. By repeatedly simulating the optimal response to
hypothetical attacks, the user is prepared to face them for real.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: With that in mind, the closest equivalent I can think of is...

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
I see! The "blaze fierce" is the determination to beat opponents.
"Water still" is the composure to assess the situation!

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
And the "katas" that balance them in the world of racing... They're the
various strategies we practice to adapt to unique race conditions!

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
That means... If I study the strategies, I'll be able to wield the blaze
with water's poise!

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text: This is a great revelation. For now, my path is clear.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text: ...I must learn from the wisdom of one experienced in the field.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
As I understand, the title of "Trainer" is granted to those with deep
knowledge of racing.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text: I ask that you continue to lend me your guidance.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
Please teach me the katas—the many strategies of Umamusume
racing. I wish to study them all!

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
Thank you. I hear sincerity in your voice... and it rings true in my
heart.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
It remains to be seen whether strategies alone will give Yaeno Muteki
the balance she seeks, but for now, they're definitely worth
practicing!
